_note: MOCK procedure result in the style of synthetic-gi-mock-v1. No real patient. For PreChart/LeftBehind development/eval.

procedure.name: Esophagogastroduodenoscopy (EGD)
procedure.indication: Persistent GERD labeled refractory to PPI; evaluate for erosive esophagitis and Barrett's esophagus. No alarm features (no dysphagia, weight loss, or GI bleeding).
procedure.prep_quality: N/A (upper endoscopy); NPO >8 hours, adequate mucosal visualization

procedure_report: PROCEDURE: Esophagogastroduodenoscopy. INDICATION: persistent GERD, rule out erosive disease/Barrett's; ongoing regular NSAID (ibuprofen) use noted. SEDATION: moderate (midazolam 2 mg, fentanyl 75 mcg). The endoscope was passed under direct visualization from the mouth to the second portion of the duodenum. ESOPHAGUS: Several longitudinal mucosal breaks were seen in the distal esophagus, non-confluent, involving more than one mucosal fold — consistent with LA Grade B erosive esophagitis. The squamocolumnar junction was at 39 cm and the diaphragmatic hiatus at 42 cm, consistent with a small (approximately 3 cm) sliding hiatal hernia. No definite salmon-colored columnar mucosa suggestive of Barrett's above the gastroesophageal junction; no mass, no stricture, and the scope traversed the GE junction without resistance. STOMACH: Mild antral erythema consistent with gastritis; no ulcer, no mass. Retroflexion normal. DUODENUM: Bulb and D2 normal; no ulcer or erosion. BIOPSIES: Distal esophageal biopsies obtained to evaluate the mucosal breaks and exclude intestinal metaplasia (specimen A); gastric antral biopsies obtained for histology/H. pylori (specimen B). No active bleeding. IMPRESSION: LA Grade B erosive esophagitis, small hiatal hernia, mild antral gastritis. Findings consistent with undertreated acid reflux compounded by NSAID use. Pathology pending.

findings.finding: Erosive esophagitis, LA Grade B (non-confluent mucosal breaks, distal esophagus)
No endoscopic evidence of Barrett's esophagus

disposition: Discharged home same day in stable condition with escort. Tolerated procedure well. Post-sedation and diet instructions reviewed.

followup.recommendation: Review distal esophageal biopsy pathology to confirm reflux esophagitis and exclude intestinal metaplasia (Barrett's); communicate result to patient
Review gastric antral biopsy pathology (histology/H. pylori); treat if positive
Optimize acid suppression: pantoprazole 40 mg BID taken 30-60 minutes before meals, reinforce adherence; discontinue/minimize ibuprofen and substitute acetaminophen
Repeat EGD in 8-12 weeks after optimized PPI therapy to confirm mucosal healing of LA Grade B esophagitis and to assess the GE junction for Barrett's on a healed mucosa

planted_followup_items.description: Distal esophageal biopsy pending — must confirm erosive esophagitis and, importantly, exclude intestinal metaplasia (Barrett's esophagus); result drives whether Barrett's surveillance is needed
planted_followup_items.expected_action: review path, call patient with result, document Barrett's status and any surveillance interval

planted_followup_items.description: Gastric antral biopsy pending — histology and H. pylori (prior stool antigen negative; confirm on tissue)
planted_followup_items.expected_action: review path; if H. pylori positive, initiate eradication therapy

planted_followup_items.description: Follow-up EGD in 8-12 weeks after optimized PPI therapy to confirm healing of LA Grade B erosive esophagitis and reassess GE junction for Barrett's on healed mucosa
planted_followup_items.expected_action: schedule surveillance/healing EGD once PPI optimized

planted_followup_items.description: Medication reconciliation and optimization (no anticoagulant/antiplatelet held for this EGD): correct PPI to BID pre-meal dosing, discontinue famotidine (already stopped by patient), and stop/minimize OTC ibuprofen (contributor to erosive disease); substitute acetaminophen for headaches
planted_followup_items.expected_action: update med list, counsel patient, coordinate NSAID cessation with PCP

planted_followup_items.description: Small sliding hiatal hernia noted — contributes to reflux; relevant if symptoms persist despite optimized medical therapy (consider anti-reflux surgery evaluation)
planted_followup_items.expected_action: document; revisit surgical evaluation only if refractory after true PPI optimization

post_procedure_followup._note: Patient-reported stream after the procedure — the inbound signal the LeftBehind product triages. Each contact is labeled ground truth so triage can be scored.

contacts.verbatim: My throat's a bit scratchy and I've been really burpy and bloated since yesterday. Is that normal?
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-EGD sore throat plus insufflation-related bloating/belching
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; mild sore throat and air-related bloating are expected after upper endoscopy and self-resolve within 24-48h. Warm fluids, lozenges as needed.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-endoscopy symptoms after a diagnostic EGD with biopsies only; no escalation.

contacts.verbatim: When will I find out what the biopsies showed? And did they see anything bad in my esophagus?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking about pending esophageal/gastric pathology and results communication
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the result: EGD showed treatable acid-reflux inflammation (LA Grade B esophagitis) and no visible Barrett's or mass; biopsies pending (expected ~2026-08-11), team will call with results and any surveillance plan.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the procedure report and follow-up plan; maps to the pending esophageal pathology item.

contacts.verbatim: I've got one of my headaches coming on — am I okay to take my ibuprofen, or should I be avoiding it now?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient clarifying NSAID use given its role in her erosive esophagitis; medication-optimization counseling opportunity
contacts.expected_action: Counsel to avoid/minimize ibuprofen because it contributed to the esophageal inflammation; use acetaminophen for headaches and follow up with PCP on headache pattern; reinforce pre-meal BID PPI dosing.
contacts.rationale: Benign, answerable medication question that maps to the medication-reconciliation/optimization follow-up item; no held anticoagulant is involved, so no bleeding-risk escalation.
